text.title: voice-ui Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for voice-ui as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLM-based VUI confirms an action ("Done!") without verifying the back-end succeeded.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Latency and API errors mean the action may have failed; hallucinated confirmations erode trust irreparably.

failure-mode.description: Total round-trip latency over 1.5 seconds without streaming audio.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Users interpret gaps beyond 1.5 s as system failure and start speaking again, causing overlapping ASR input.

failure-mode.description: Utterance logs retained without a privacy/PII policy defined in the design phase.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Voice utterances contain sensitive context; retention policy must be a design constraint, not an afterthought.

failure-mode.description: No "endpointing" visual cue — the user cannot see if the system is listening.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a visible or auditory listening indicator, users do not know when to speak, causing silence timeouts.
